iast: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
eng: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits.

dev: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
iast: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dhanañjaya
eng: Established in yoga, perform actions, abandoning attachment, Dhananjaya.

iast: duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ
eng: Whose mind is undisturbed in suffering, whose longing for pleasures has gone.

iast: uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet
eng: One should lift oneself by oneself; one should not degrade oneself.

iast: yato yato niścarati manaś cañcalam asthiram
eng: Wheresoever the restless, unsteady mind strays.

dev: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।
iast: adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṃ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca
eng: He who hates none, who is friendly and compassionate to all beings.

iast: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja
eng: Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone; I will free you from all evils.

dev: ईशा वास्यमिदꣳ सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्।
iast: īśā vāsyam idaṃ sarvaṃ yat kiñca jagatyāṃ jagat
eng: The Lord inhabits all this — whatever moves in this moving world.

dev: श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रं मनसो मनो यद्वाचो ह वाचः स उ प्राणस्य प्राणः।
iast: śrotasya śrotraṃ manaso mano yad vāco ha vācaḥ sa u prāṇasya prāṇaḥ
eng: The ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the speech of speech, the breath of breath.

iast: aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmāsya jantor nihito guhāyām
eng: Smaller than the small, greater than the great, the Self rests hidden in the heart of every creature.

iast: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varān nibodhata
eng: Arise, awake, having approached the wise, know this.

dev: द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते।
iast: dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṃ vṛkṣaṃ pariṣasvajāte
eng: Two birds, companions, cling to the same tree — one eats the fruit, the other looks on.

iast: tat tvam asi śvetaketo
eng: That thou art, O Śvetaketu.

iast: asato mā sad gamaya
eng: Lead me from the unreal to the real.

iast: yaḥ pṛthivyāṃ tiṣṭhan pṛthivyā antaraḥ
eng: He who dwells in earth, yet is within earth — whom earth does not know.

iast: satyaṃ jñānam anantaṃ brahma
eng: Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinite.

iast: yogaś citta-vṛtti-nirodhaḥ
eng: Yoga is the stilling of the movements of the mind.

iast: maitrī-karuṇā-muditopekṣāṇāṃ sukha-duḥkha-puṇya-apuṇya-viṣayāṇāṃ bhāvanātaś citta-prasādanam
eng: Clarity of mind comes from cultivating friendliness, compassion, joy, and equanimity.

iast: ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate
eng: Those who think of none else, who worship Me with devotion — I carry what they lack.

iast: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
eng: The wise see the same in a learned brāhmaṇa, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.

iast: yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate ca yaḥ
eng: He by whom the world is not troubled and who is not troubled by the world.

iast: yathā sarva-gataṃ saukṣmyād ākāśaṃ nopalipyate
eng: As the all-pervading sky is not stained because of its subtlety, so the Self is not stained by the body.

dev: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।
iast: sarvasya cāhaṃ hṛdi sanniviṣṭo mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṃ ca
eng: I am seated in the hearts of all; from Me come memory, knowledge, and their loss.

iast: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṃ kāryaṃ karma samācara
eng: Therefore, without attachment, always perform the action that must be done.

dev: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
iast: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
eng: Whenever there is a decline of dharma and a rise of adharma, I manifest Myself.

iast: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām
eng: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of the wicked, for the establishment of dharma.

iast: aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarva-bhūtāśaya-sthitaḥ
eng: I am the Self, O Guḍākeśa, seated in the hearts of all beings.

dev: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्।
iast: tasmāt tvam uttiṣṭha yaśo labhasva jitvā śatrūn bhuṅkṣva rājyaṃ samṛddham
eng: Therefore arise, win glory, conquer your foes, and enjoy a flourishing kingdom.

iast: trividhaṃ narakasyedaṃ dvāraṃ nāśanam ātmanaḥ
eng: This is the threefold gate of hell, destructive of the self: lust, anger, and greed.

dev: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।
iast: anudvega-karaṃ vākyaṃ satyaṃ priya-hitaṃ ca yat
eng: Speech that causes no anxiety, which is true and agreeable and beneficial.

iast: yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat
eng: Whatever you do, eat, offer, or give — do it as an offering to Me.

iast: mātra-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
eng: Contacts with matter, O son of Kuntī, give rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain.
